records.ety: [L. chrysolithos, Gr. <?/; chryso`s gold + <?/ stone: cf. F. chrysolithe.]

defs: A mineral, composed of silica, magnesia, and iron, of a yellow to green color. It is common in certain volcanic rocks; -- called also olivine and peridot. Sometimes used as a gem. The name was also early used for yellow varieties of tourmaline and topaz.
